title: Quantum Error Correction

theory.introduction: Quantum Error Correction (QEC) protects quantum information from noise and decoherence. Unlike classical error correction, QEC must preserve superposition and entanglement while detecting and correcting errors without directly measuring the quantum state.
key_points: Quantum information is highly sensitive to noise
QEC encodes logical qubit into multiple physical qubits
Syndrome measurements detect errors without collapsing state
Error correction enables reliable quantum computation

core_idea.description: Encode one logical qubit into multiple physical qubits so errors can be detected and corrected.

steps: Encode logical qubit into 3 physical qubits

shor_code.description: Combines bit flip and phase flip protection
shor_code.importance: First complete quantum error correction code

syndrome_measurement.description: Ancilla qubits measure parity information without collapsing logical state.
syndrome_measurement.benefit: Detect errors indirectly without destroying superposition

circuit_examples.name: 3-Qubit Bit Flip Encoding
circuit_examples.description: Encode logical qubit using CNOT gates

logical_vs_physical.physical_qubits: Multiple qubits used to encode logical qubit

importance: Required for fault tolerant quantum computing

connection_to_noise.relationship: QEC methods are designed to counteract noise models such as bit flip and phase flip errors.

summary: Quantum Error Correction enables reliable quantum computation by encoding logical qubits into multiple physical qubits and correcting errors without destroying quantum information.

simulation.expected_result: |0⟩ — error detected and corrected by majority vote
try_this: First two CNOTs encode the logical qubit into all 3 physical qubits
X on qubit 1 simulates a bit-flip error on one physical qubit
Final CNOTs perform syndrome measurement to detect and correct the error

quiz.question: Why can't we simply copy a qubit for error correction?
options: The no-cloning theorem prevents copying an unknown quantum state
quiz.answer: The no-cloning theorem prevents copying an unknown quantum state
quiz.explanation: Quantum mechanics forbids exact copying of an unknown quantum state, so QEC uses entanglement instead.

quiz.question: How many physical qubits does the 3-qubit bit-flip code use to protect 1 logical qubit?
quiz.explanation: The 3-qubit bit-flip code encodes 1 logical qubit into 3 physical qubits: α|000⟩ + β|111⟩.

quiz.question: What do syndrome measurements detect?
options: Which qubit experienced an error, without collapsing the logical state
quiz.answer: Which qubit experienced an error, without collapsing the logical state
quiz.explanation: Syndrome measurements use ancilla qubits to measure parity information, detecting errors without measuring the logical state.

quiz.question: How many physical qubits does the Shor code use?
quiz.explanation: The Shor code requires 9 physical qubits and can correct both bit-flip and phase-flip errors.
